reason: Response A is a tighter, more polished one-liner with a clean dual meaning — "ducking" works both as the autocorrect joke and as a descriptor for the punchline phrase "know-it-all." Response B's structure is looser and the setup ("Why autocorrect will never understand you") is more of a headline than a one-liner, and the payoff is weaker. Claude Opus tends to favor economy and precision in wordplay, making A the more characteristic output.
